injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned to Manchester City training week of May 4-7; available for end-of-season fixtures, expected fully fit for tournament

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Suffered hamstring injury in second half; subsequent ankle issue extended absence beyond original 4-6 week timeline

injuryUpdates.source: Sky Sports, ESPN, Sports Mole, Yahoo Sports - April/May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Originally hamstring (Jan 5 vs Chelsea), compounded by ankle issue. Out roughly four months. Back in Man City training as of May 7 alongside Gvardiol. Likely fit for the tournament but match sharpness a concern. Bumped from prior 'fit' status as the older entry pre-dated the January setback being relevant to current state.

injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Returned to PSG training late April ahead of Champions League semifinal vs Bayern Munich

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Picked up heel inflammation; missed PSG matches vs Nantes and Angers

injuryUpdates.source: The Portugal Post, beIN Sports, Goal.com - April 21-27, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Brief heel scare in mid-April caused him to miss two Ligue 1 games. Returned to full PSG training before the Champions League semifinal first leg vs Bayern. No expected impact on tournament availability — Vitinha is the engine of Portugal's midfield and the Ballon d'Or third-place finisher. Worth tracking given PSG's deep Champions League run loads minutes.

squadChanges.details: Toluca striker Paulinho was called up to the March camp as a replacement for the injured Rafael Leao and has stayed in the picture for the preliminary 35-55 player list submitted on May 11. Provides additional forward depth alongside Goncalo Ramos and Joao Felix.

Turkey-based goalkeeper Ricardo Velho (27) was added to the goalkeeper pool after Diogo Costa's adductor/back injury. Yet to earn his first senior cap. Likely competes with Rui Silva and Jose Sa for the backup spot behind Costa.

bettingOddsUpdate.source: BetMGM / Squawka / Lineups.com / bet365 aggregate — May 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Portugal priced around +1100 (~8% implied) as the 6th favorite behind Spain, France, England, Brazil, Argentina. Drifted in from +1400 pre-Nations League final to +1200 after winning it, then to +1100 currently. BetMGM lists Portugal among its largest tournament liabilities (4th in bet count, 2nd in money wagered). Group-specific markets (advancing from Group K / group-stage exit) not surfaced in aggregator scan — Portugal expected to top Group K comfortably given opposition (COL, COD, UZB).

summary: Portugal's biggest delta since April 16 is the Ruben Dias situation: the City centre-back, listed previously as fully fit, has in reality been out since January 5 with overlapping hamstring/ankle issues and only just returned to training in early May — likely available for the tournament but short on match sharpness. Vitinha had a brief heel scare in mid-April but is back training at PSG. Roberto Martinez's preliminary 35-55 player list went to the governing body on May 11, with the final 26 due to be announced May 19; Ricardo Velho and Paulinho remain in the picture as cover for the injured Diogo Costa and Rafael Leao respectively. No new international matches have been played in the window; the next test is Chile in Lisbon on June 6 followed by Nigeria in Leiria on June 10.
